2019-06-25.abstract: The cases will be closely watched by foreign governments troubled by President Recep Tayyip Erdogan’s efforts to crack down on the opposition.

2019-06-25.title: 1894: President Carnot of France Assassinated
2019-06-25.abstract: The president of the French Republic was fatally stabbed as he left a banquet at the Palais du Commerce in Lyon.

2019-06-25.title: Tired of Hearing Leader Criticized, Pakistani Lawmaker Starts Banning Words
2019-06-25.abstract: After the opposition kept calling Prime Minister Imran Khan a leader “selected” by the country’s military, the deputy speaker banned the description. The debate is not over.

2019-06-25.title: Trump Threatens ‘Obliteration’ of Iran, as Sanctions Dispute Escalates
2019-06-25.abstract: President Hassan Rouhani vowed that Tehran would not be intimidated. His words drew a blistering warning from the White House.

2019-06-25.title: Duterte Plays Down Chinese Ramming of Philippine Fishing Boat
2019-06-25.abstract: The Philippine president warned against escalating tensions with China over the incident, declaring that “there was no confrontation” and adding, “Well, I’m sorry, but that’s how it is.”

2019-06-26.title: European Heat Wave? It’s the New Normal.
2019-06-26.abstract: Meteorologists say temperatures could climb above 40 degrees Celsius, or more than 104 degrees Fahrenheit, across large stretches of the continent.

2019-06-26.title: A Mystery Disease Is Killing Children, and Questions Linger About Lychees
2019-06-26.abstract: Researchers said the fruit was behind annual outbreaks of a fatal syndrome in eastern India. But local doctors say that theory can’t explain all the cases.

2019-06-26.title: 2 U.S. Soldiers Killed in Fierce Firefight in Afghanistan
2019-06-26.abstract: The latest American military deaths, bringing this year’s total to nine in the country, come days before the next round of peace talks between the United States and the Taliban.

2019-06-26.title: Why Hong Kong’s Protesters Are Turning to G-20 Leaders for Help
2019-06-26.abstract: Beijing has said that its leaders will not discuss Hong Kong at the Group of 20 summit meeting. But Hong Kong protesters still hope for global backing.

2019-06-26.title: Boris Johnson, Political Escape Artist, Lands in Hot Water. Again.
2019-06-26.abstract: The front-runner to become the British prime minister is frequently caught in gaffes, oversights and outright lies. And that’s what his fans like about him.

2019-06-27.title: At G20, Trump Seeks Fresh Start With Putin After End of Mueller Inquiry
2019-06-27.abstract: President Trump’s advisers hope to use the session to pivot the relationship toward arms control, a paradoxical priority given the administration’s recent moves to dismantle treaties.

2019-06-27.title: Australian Student Is Missing in North Korea, His Family Says
2019-06-27.abstract: The family said that it had not heard in days from Alek Sigley, 29, who has offered a close look at his life inside the reclusive North on social media.

2019-06-27.title: Trump Once Again Assails America’s Friends as He Opens Overseas Visit
2019-06-27.abstract: President Trump complained that Japan would sit back if the United States were attacked and watch “on a Sony television.” He also assailed Germany and India.

2019-06-27.title: A School With No Heat or Computers but Many College-Bound Students. Mostly Girls.
2019-06-27.abstract: At an Afghan mountain school, there are no computers, lights or heat. Even books are few. Yet 90 percent of graduates get into college. Most of them are girls.

2019-06-27.title: Angela Merkel Is Seen Shaking Again, but Spokesman Insists She’s Fine
2019-06-27.abstract: The German chancellor began trembling at a ceremony in Berlin on Thursday, 10 days after she blamed high temperatures for another shaking episode.

2019-06-28.title: As New York Celebrates Pride, Australia Debates Freedom
2019-06-28.abstract: The case of Israel Folau, a Christian rugby player whose contract was canceled over his anti-gay posts, has spurred a national rift over how rights coexist.

2019-06-28.title: Before Talks With Trump, Xi Promotes His Image as a Leader With Friends
2019-06-28.abstract: At the G20 summit, Mr. Xi needs to be shown as pursuing a bigger agenda who can offset animosities with the United States.

2019-06-28.title: Last 2 Towers of Genoa Bridge That Collapsed, Killing 43, Are Demolished
2019-06-28.abstract: The government has pledged to rebuild the structure in a year. Area residents and business owners are eager for the construction to be completed.

2019-06-28.title: What Do the Taliban Want in Afghanistan? A Lost Constitution Offers Clues
2019-06-28.abstract: Deep resistance to democracy and protections for minorities within the Taliban code will emerge as huge hurdles if the insurgents seek agreement with the Afghan government.

2019-06-28.title: Trump and Putin Share Joke About Election Meddling, Sparking New Furor
2019-06-28.abstract: At the leaders’ first formal meeting in a year, President Trump again made clear that he does not take Russia’s electoral interference as seriously as others do.

2019-06-29.title: American Missiles Found in Libyan Rebel Compound
2019-06-29.abstract: The powerful American missiles had fallen into the hands of rebels fighting a U.S.-backed government. The U.S. appears to have sold the missiles to the U.A.E.

2019-06-29.title: Trump Offers Kim Jong-un a Last-Minute Invitation to Meet at the DMZ
2019-06-29.abstract: President Trump said in a Twitter post that he would be happy to greet Mr. Kim across the line that has divided Korea for nearly 75 years.

2019-06-29.title: Italy Arrests Captain of Ship That Rescued Dozens of Migrants at Sea
2019-06-29.abstract: Capt. Carola Rackete, an outlaw to some and a heroine to others, was seized after her ship, the Sea Watch 3, docked in Lampedusa and 42 migrants disembarked.

2019-06-29.title: One Casualty of the Palm Oil Industry: An Orangutan Mother, Shot 74 Times
2019-06-29.abstract: Indonesia has promised to stop clearing jungle for plantations. So why are endangered apes still on the front lines of the conservation battle?

2019-06-29.title: Boycott by Whites of South African Restaurant Reflects Growing Sense of Grievance
2019-06-29.abstract: Many Afrikaners are spurning a once-cherished restaurant chain to protest its banning of a white man. The boycott has drawn “an eye roll” among many black South Africans.

2019-06-30.title: Venezuelan Navy Captain Accused of Rebellion Dies After Signs of Torture
2019-06-30.abstract: The death underlined President Nicolás Maduro’s increasingly ferocious repression campaign amid a spiraling economic crisis.

2019-06-30.title: Trump Steps Into North Korea and Agrees With Kim Jong-un to Resume Talks
2019-06-30.abstract: American and North Korean leaders shook hands at the line separating the peninsula for a camera-friendly demonstration of friendship intended to revitalize nuclear negotiations.

2019-06-30.title: A Monster to History, Stalin Is a Tourist Magnet in His Hometown
2019-06-30.abstract: How do you market a homegrown monster to the rest of the world? A popular museum in Stalin’s hometown in Georgia adopts an admiring tone, and the guided tour skips over his millions of victims.

2019-06-30.title: Did Hong Kong Police Abuse Protesters? What Videos Show
2019-06-30.abstract: Tear-gassed, beaten and dragged. Experts in crowd control say the Hong Kong police used excessive force on protesters during a demonstration in June.

2019-06-30.title: Dozens Killed as Taliban Bombs in 4 Humvees Rip Through Afghan District
2019-06-30.abstract: Insurgents rammed the vehicles full of explosives into a compound that the government had moved once after militant fighters took over its original location.
